1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Code Graph Index Scope Policy
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: 
5: const SEGMENT_BOUNDARY = '(^|/)';
6: 
7: function compileSegmentPattern(segment: string): RegExp {
8:   return new RegExp(`${SEGMENT_BOUNDARY}${segment}(/|$)`, 'i');
9: }
10: 
11: function normalizeIndexScopePath(filePath: string | null | undefined): string {
12:   if (!filePath || typeof filePath !== 'string') return '';
13:   return filePath.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/\/+/g, '/');
14: }
15: 
16: function matchesAnyPattern(filePath: string, patterns: readonly RegExp[]): boolean {
17:   const normalizedPath = normalizeIndexScopePath(filePath);
18:   return patterns.some(pattern => pattern.test(normalizedPath));
19: }
20: 
21: function getOpencodeSegment(filePath: string, segment: string): string | null {
22:   const normalizedPath = normalizeIndexScopePath(filePath);
23:   const match = normalizedPath.match(new RegExp(`(^|/)\\.opencode/${segment}/([^/]+)(/|$)`, 'i'));
24:   return match?.[2] ?? null;
25: }
26: 
27: function isAllowedByBooleanScope(filePath: string, segment: string, enabled: unknown): boolean {
28:   return getOpencodeSegment(filePath, segment) === null || enabled === true;
29: }
30: 
31: function isAllowedSkill(filePath: string, policy: Record<string, unknown>): boolean {
32:   const skillName = getOpencodeSegment(filePath, 'skills');
33:   if (!skillName) {
34:     return true;
35:   }
36: 
37:   const includedSkillsList = policy.includedSkillsList;
38:   if (includedSkillsList === 'all') {
39:     return true;
40:   }
41:   if (Array.isArray(includedSkillsList)) {
42:     return includedSkillsList.includes(skillName);
43:   }
44:   return false;
45: }
46: 
47: export const EXCLUDED_FOR_CODE_GRAPH = [
48:   compileSegmentPattern('external'),
49:   compileSegmentPattern('node_modules'),
50:   compileSegmentPattern('\\.git'),
51:   compileSegmentPattern('dist'),
52:   compileSegmentPattern('vendor'),
53:   compileSegmentPattern('z_future'),
54:   compileSegmentPattern('z_archive'),
55: ] as const;
56: 
57: export function shouldIndexForCodeGraph(
58:   absolutePath: string,
59:   policyInput?: unknown,
60: ): boolean {
61:   if (matchesAnyPattern(absolutePath, EXCLUDED_FOR_CODE_GRAPH)) {
62:     return false;
63:   }
64: 
65:   if (!policyInput || typeof policyInput !== 'object') {
66:     return true;
67:   }
68: 
69:   const policy = policyInput as Record<string, unknown>;
70:   return isAllowedSkill(absolutePath, policy)
71:     && isAllowedByBooleanScope(absolutePath, 'agents', policy.includeAgents)
72:     && isAllowedByBooleanScope(absolutePath, 'commands', policy.includeCommands)
73:     && isAllowedByBooleanScope(absolutePath, 'specs', policy.includeSpecs)
74:     && isAllowedByBooleanScope(absolutePath, 'plugins', policy.includePlugins);
75: }

  Line 4: trigger_phrases:

  Line 110: A daemon watches every `SKILL.md` and `graph-metadata.json` under `.opencode/skills/`. When a watched source changes, the daemon schedules an incremental reindex, publishes a fresh generation after the rebuild, and invalidates the recommendation cache. It still holds a single-writer lease; explicit trusted mutation paths remain `advisor_rebuild` and `skill_graph_scan`, and corrupt-database recovery may also move aside and recreate the database during lazy initialization.

  Line 119: | `unavailable` | The subsystem cannot be reached | Fall back to `skill_advisor.py` or keyword matching against frontmatter `trigger_phrases` |

  Line 127: With `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS=true`, `skill_graph_scan` also harvests frontmatter (`title`, `description`, `trigger_phrases`, `importance_tier`, `contextType`) from every markdown file under each skill's `references/` and `assets/` (READMEs excluded) into a `skill_docs` table, and the watcher registers those docs so edits re-index the owning skill. Doc phrases score inside the `derived_generated` lane — top-3 docs per skill, tier-weighted, contribution capped at 0.45 — so they assist ranking but cannot hard-route alone. Matching recommendations carry an optional `matchedDocs` field (max 3 sanitized skill-relative paths) pointing at the exact doc to open. Flag unset (the default) changes nothing: no harvest, no watch targets, identical scoring. Deployment note: the launcher forwards only allowlisted env to the daemon child, so the flag works because it sits in `CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST` (`mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs`) — any future advisor env flag needs the same entry.

  Line 131: The `semantic_shadow` lane runs against a pluggable embedder layer shared with `mk-spec-memory`. The registry holds text-tuned embedding models and defaults through a local-first cascade to a local model when no embedder is explicitly set. For the full model registry, the cascade tier table and the swap workflow, see `INSTALL_GUIDE.md` §12.

/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skills/system-skill-advisor/changelog/v0.8.0.md:
  Line 1: This release gives the detailed frontmatter on skill reference/asset docs a real runtime consumer. With `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS=true`, every `skill_graph_scan` harvests `title`, `description`, `trigger_phrases`, `importance_tier` and `contextType` from each skill's `references/` and `assets/` markdown into a new `skill_docs` table, the daemon watcher tracks those docs for freshness, and the scorer turns their phrases into dampened routing signal inside the existing `derived_generated` lane. Doc-specific prompts now rank the owning skill AND point at the exact file to open via an optional `matchedDocs` response field. The flag defaults off, and flag-off behavior is byte-identical to v0.7.0 — that invariance is the hard contract in the new test file.

  Line 3: > Spec folder: `.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/145-advisor-doc-trigger-harvest/` (contract origin: `system-spec-kit/027-xce-research-based-refinement/000-release-cleanup/009-skill-frontmatter-alignment/001-frontmatter-benefit-investigation/`)

  Line 11: - **`skill_docs` table** -- additive DDL (schema + migration), UNIQUE per skill+doc path, FK-cascade with skill deletion. The harvest runs for every skill on every scan — doc edits never touch `graph-metadata.json`, so node-level hash skips cannot gate doc work — and stays cheap via per-doc content hashes. Stale rows are swept per skill; unreadable docs warn (`DOC-READ-FAILED`) and continue.

  Line 13: - **Watcher freshness** -- each harvestable doc becomes a `doc-frontmatter` watch target when the flag is on, so editing a reference re-indexes its skill without a manual scan.

  Line 18: - **`matchedDocs` on the strict schema** -- max 3 skill-relative paths, extracted from `doc:` evidence behind a sanitization allowlist (references/assets prefix, markdown only, traversal rejected); new `doc_reference_signal` evidence type.

  Line 23: - **Launcher env allowlist** -- the same smoke caught the daemon child never receiving the flag: `mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs` forwards only `CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST` keys, and `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS` is now on that list. Any future advisor env flag needs the same entry to reach the daemon.

  Line 29: | `mcp_server/lib/skill-graph/doc-frontmatter.ts` | New leaf module: flag gate, tier weights, list-aware parser, capped walker |

  Line 30: | `mcp_server/lib/skill-graph/skill-graph-db.ts` | `skill_docs` DDL + migration, `harvestSkillDocs()`, scan-result `docs` counters |

  Line 33: | `mcp_server/schemas/advisor-tool-schemas.ts`, `mcp_server/handlers/advisor-recommend.ts` | Optional `matchedDocs` (max 3) + sanitized extraction + `doc_reference_signal` |

  Line 34: | `mcp_server/lib/daemon/watcher.ts` | `doc-frontmatter` watch targets when the flag is on |

  Line 36: | `.opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs` | `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS` added to `CHILD_ENV_ALLOWLIST` |

  Line 41: No migration required: the table is created additively on open, and with the flag unset nothing changes. To enable, set `SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS=true` in the advisor env block of each runtime config (this repo sets it in `opencode.json`, `.claude/mcp.json` and `.codex/config.toml`), then adopt via fresh session — the advisor launcher exits if its daemon child is SIGTERMed, and launchers spawned before the flag flip keep their old env, so every advisor-attached session must end before one fresh session respawns the daemon with the flag. Verify with a trusted `skill_graph_scan` (expect `docs` counters) and an `advisor_recommend` for a doc-specific phrase (expect `matchedDocs`). Doc coverage grows with the skill-frontmatter authoring campaign (84 of ~355 docs carry `trigger_phrases` at release). Rollback: unset the flag (rows persist, ignored) or drop the `skill_docs` table.

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Skill Doc Frontmatter Harvest
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Parses the canonical reference/asset frontmatter contract
5: // (title, description, trigger_phrases, importance_tier, contextType)
6: // so per-doc trigger phrases can feed advisor routing as doc-level
7: // signal. The Spec Kit Memory system deliberately never indexes these
8: // files; the advisor is their only runtime consumer.
9: 
10: import { readdirSync, statSync } from 'node:fs';
11: import { join } from 'node:path';
12: 
13: export interface ParsedDocFrontmatter {
14:   readonly title: string;
15:   readonly description: string;
16:   readonly triggerPhrases: readonly string[];
17:   readonly importanceTier: string;
18:   readonly contextType: string;
19: }
20: 
21: /** Opt-in gate for the whole doc-harvest path. Default off. */
22: export function isDocTriggerHarvestEnabled(env: NodeJS.ProcessEnv = process.env): boolean {
23:   return env.SPECKIT_ADVISOR_DOC_TRIGGERS?.trim().toLowerCase() === 'true';
24: }
25: 
26: // Mirrors the spec-kit importance vocabulary so doc authors reuse one
27: // tier language. Values dampen routing contribution, never boost above
28: // the skill's own curated signal.
29: const DOC_TIER_WEIGHTS: Readonly<Record<string, number>> = {
30:   constitutional: 1.0,
31:   critical: 1.0,
32:   important: 0.85,
33:   normal: 0.7,
34:   temporary: 0.4,
35:   deprecated: 0.2,
36: };
37: 
38: const DEFAULT_TIER = 'normal';
39: 
40: export function docTierWeight(tier: string | null | undefined): number {
41:   const normalized = (tier ?? DEFAULT_TIER).trim().toLowerCase();
42:   return DOC_TIER_WEIGHTS[normalized] ?? DOC_TIER_WEIGHTS[DEFAULT_TIER];
43: }
44: 
45: const SURROUNDING_QUOTES = /^["']|["']$/g;
46: const KEY_LINE = /^([A-Za-z0-9_-]+):\s*(.*)$/;
47: const LIST_ITEM = /^\s+-\s+(.*)$/;
48: const MAX_PHRASES_PER_DOC = 12;
49: const MAX_FIELD_LENGTH = 300;
50: 
51: function cleanScalar(raw: string): string {
52:   return raw.trim().replace(SURROUNDING_QUOTES, '').trim().slice(0, MAX_FIELD_LENGTH);
53: }
54: 
55: function splitInlineList(inner: string): string[] {
56:   const entries: string[] = [];
57:   let current = '';
58:   let quote: '"' | "'" | null = null;
59:   let escaped = false;
60:   for (const char of inner) {
61:     if (escaped) {
62:       current += char;
63:       escaped = false;
64:       continue;
65:     }
66:     if (char === '\\' && quote) {
67:       current += char;
68:       escaped = true;
69:       continue;
70:     }
71:     if ((char === '"' || char === "'") && (!quote || quote === char)) {
72:       quote = quote ? null : char;
73:       current += char;
74:       continue;
75:     }
76:     if (char === ',' && !quote) {
77:       entries.push(current);
78:       current = '';
79:       continue;
80:     }
81:     current += char;
82:   }
83:   if (current.trim()) entries.push(current);
84:   return entries;
85: }
86: 
87: /**
88:  * Parse the YAML-ish frontmatter block of a skill reference/asset doc.
89:  *
90:  * Returns null when the doc has no frontmatter fence or no
91:  * `trigger_phrases` entries — docs without the detailed contract carry
92:  * no doc-level routing signal and are skipped, not errored.
93:  */
94: export function parseDocFrontmatter(raw: string): ParsedDocFrontmatter | null {
95:   if (!raw.startsWith('---\n') && !raw.startsWith('---\r\n')) return null;
96:   const end = raw.indexOf('\n---', 3);
97:   if (end <= 3) return null;
98:   const block = raw.slice(raw.indexOf('\n') + 1, end);
99: 
100:   const scalars: Record<string, string> = {};
101:   const phrases: string[] = [];
102:   let inPhraseList = false;
103: 
104:   for (const line of block.split(/\r?\n/)) {
105:     const listMatch = LIST_ITEM.exec(line);
106:     if (listMatch) {
107:       if (inPhraseList && phrases.length < MAX_PHRASES_PER_DOC) {
108:         const phrase = cleanScalar(listMatch[1]);
109:         if (phrase) phrases.push(phrase);
110:       }
111:       continue;
112:     }
113: 
114:     const keyMatch = KEY_LINE.exec(line);
115:     if (!keyMatch) continue;
116:     const key = keyMatch[1];
117:     const value = keyMatch[2].trim();
118:     inPhraseList = key === 'trigger_phrases';
119: 
120:     if (key === 'trigger_phrases' && value.startsWith('[')) {
121:       // Inline list form: trigger_phrases: ["a", "b"]
122:       const inner = value.replace(/^\[|\]$/g, '');
123:       for (const entry of splitInlineList(inner)) {
124:         if (phrases.length >= MAX_PHRASES_PER_DOC) break;
125:         const phrase = cleanScalar(entry);
126:         if (phrase) phrases.push(phrase);
127:       }
128:       inPhraseList = false;
129:       continue;
130:     }
131: 
132:     if (value) scalars[key] = cleanScalar(value);
133:   }
134: 
135:   if (phrases.length === 0) return null;
136: 
137:   return {
138:     title: scalars.title ?? '',
139:     description: scalars.description ?? '',
140:     triggerPhrases: phrases,
141:     importanceTier: (scalars.importance_tier ?? DEFAULT_TIER).toLowerCase(),
142:     contextType: (scalars.contextType ?? scalars.context_type ?? 'general').toLowerCase(),
143:   };
144: }
145: 
146: const HARVEST_SUBDIRS = ['references', 'assets'] as const;
147: const MAX_WALK_DEPTH = 6;
148: const MAX_DOCS_PER_SKILL = 200;
149: 
150: function isHarvestableMarkdown(name: string): boolean {
151:   const lower = name.toLowerCase();
152:   return lower.endsWith('.md') && lower !== 'readme.md';
153: }
154: 
155: function walkDocsDir(dir: string, depth: number, collected: string[]): void {
156:   if (depth > MAX_WALK_DEPTH || collected.length >= MAX_DOCS_PER_SKILL) return;
157:   let entries;
158:   try {
159:     entries = readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true });
160:   } catch {
161:     return;
162:   }
163:   for (const entry of entries) {
164:     if (collected.length >= MAX_DOCS_PER_SKILL) return;
165:     const fullPath = join(dir, entry.name);
166:     if (entry.isDirectory()) {
167:       walkDocsDir(fullPath, depth + 1, collected);
168:       continue;
169:     }
170:     if (!entry.isFile() || !isHarvestableMarkdown(entry.name)) continue;
171:     collected.push(fullPath);
172:   }
173: }
174: 
175: /**
176:  * List harvestable doc files (references/assets markdown, READMEs
177:  * excluded) for one skill directory. Paths are absolute and sorted for
178:  * deterministic indexing.
179:  */
180: export function listSkillDocFiles(skillDir: string): string[] {
181:   const collected: string[] = [];
182:   for (const subdir of HARVEST_SUBDIRS) {
183:     const root = join(skillDir, subdir);
184:     try {
185:       if (!statSync(root).isDirectory()) continue;
186:     } catch {
187:       continue;
188:     }
189:     walkDocsDir(root, 0, collected);
190:   }
191:   return collected.sort();
192: }

(End of file - total 192 lines)

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Seed Resolver
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Resolves file, manual, and graph seeds to code graph nodes.
5: // Resolution chain: exact symbol → near-exact symbol → enclosing symbol → file anchor.
6: 
7: import * as graphDb from './code-graph-db.js';
8: import type { SymbolKind } from './indexer-types.js';
9: 
10: /** A file seed from a caller-provided path and optional line range. */
11: export interface CodeGraphSeed {
12:   filePath: string;
13:   startLine?: number;
14:   endLine?: number;
15:   query?: string;
16:   source?: string;
17: }
18: 
19: /** Manual seed with symbol name (no file path required) */
20: export interface ManualSeed {
21:   provider: 'manual';
22:   symbolName: string;
23:   filePath?: string;
24:   kind?: SymbolKind;
25:   source?: string;
26: }
27: 
28: /** Pre-resolved graph node seed */
29: export interface GraphSeed {
30:   provider: 'graph';
31:   nodeId: string;
32:   symbolId: string;
33:   source?: string;
34: }
35: 
36: /** Union type for all seed kinds */
37: export type AnySeed = CodeGraphSeed | ManualSeed | GraphSeed;
38: 
39: /** A resolved reference into the code graph */
40: export interface ArtifactRef {
41:   filePath: string;
42:   startLine: number;
43:   endLine: number;
44:   symbolId: string | null;
45:   fqName: string | null;
46:   kind: string | null;
47:   confidence: number; // 0.0 - 1.0
48:   resolution: 'exact' | 'near_exact' | 'enclosing' | 'file_anchor';
49:   score: number | null;
50:   snippet: string | null;
51:   range: { start: number; end: number } | null;
52:   provider: 'graph' | 'manual' | 'code_graph';
53:   source?: string;
54: }
55: 
56: // ── Type guards ──────────────────────────────────────────────
57: 
58: function hasProvider(seed: unknown, provider: string): boolean {
59:   if (typeof seed !== 'object' || seed === null) {
60:     return false;
61:   }
62:   return (seed as { provider?: unknown }).provider === provider;
63: }
64: 
65: function isManualSeed(seed: unknown): seed is ManualSeed {
66:   return hasProvider(seed, 'manual');
67: }
68: 
69: function isGraphSeed(seed: unknown): seed is GraphSeed {
70:   return hasProvider(seed, 'graph');
71: }
72: 
73: function throwResolutionError(operation: string, seed: unknown, err: unknown): never {
74:   const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
75:   console.error(`[seed-resolver] ${operation} failed`, { seed, error: message });
76:   throw new Error(`[seed-resolver] ${operation} failed: ${message}`);
77: }
78: 
79: // ── Seed resolvers ───────────────────────────────────────────
80: 
81: /** Resolve a ManualSeed by looking up the symbol name in the DB */
82: export function resolveManualSeed(seed: ManualSeed): ArtifactRef {
83:   try {
84:     const d = graphDb.getDb();
85: 
86:     let query = 'SELECT * FROM code_nodes WHERE name = ?';
87:     const params: unknown[] = [seed.symbolName];
88: 
89:     if (seed.filePath) {
90:       query += ' AND file_path = ?';
91:       params.push(seed.filePath);
92:     }
93:     if (seed.kind) {
94:       query += ' AND kind = ?';
95:       params.push(seed.kind);
96:     }
97:     query += ' LIMIT 1';
98: 
99:     const row = d.prepare(query).get(...params) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
100: 
101:     if (row) {
102:       return {
103:         filePath: row.file_path as string,
104:         startLine: row.start_line as number,
105:         endLine: row.end_line as number,
106:         symbolId: row.symbol_id as string,
107:         fqName: row.fq_name as string,
108:         kind: row.kind as string,
109:         confidence: 0.9,
110:         resolution: 'exact',
111:         score: null,
112:         snippet: null,
113:         range: null,
114:         provider: 'manual',
115:         source: seed.source,
116:       };
117:     }
118:   } catch (err: unknown) {
119:     throwResolutionError('resolveManualSeed', seed, err);
120:   }
121: 
122:   return {
123:     filePath: seed.filePath ?? '<unknown>',
124:     startLine: 1,
125:     endLine: 1,
126:     symbolId: null,
127:     fqName: null,
128:     kind: seed.kind ?? null,
129:     confidence: 0.1,
130:     resolution: 'file_anchor',
131:     score: null,
132:     snippet: null,
133:     range: null,
134:     provider: 'manual',
135:     source: seed.source,
136:   };
137: }
138: 
139: /** Resolve a GraphSeed by looking up the node by symbolId in the DB */
140: export function resolveGraphSeed(seed: GraphSeed): ArtifactRef {
141:   try {
142:     const d = graphDb.getDb();
143: 
144:     const row = d.prepare(
145:       'SELECT * FROM code_nodes WHERE symbol_id = ? LIMIT 1'
146:     ).get(seed.symbolId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
147: 
148:     if (row) {
149:       return {
150:         filePath: row.file_path as string,
151:         startLine: row.start_line as number,
152:         endLine: row.end_line as number,
153:         symbolId: row.symbol_id as string,
154:         fqName: row.fq_name as string,
155:         kind: row.kind as string,
156:         confidence: 1.0,
157:         resolution: 'exact',
158:         score: null,
159:         snippet: null,
160:         range: null,
161:         provider: 'graph',
162:         source: seed.source,
163:       };
164:     }
165:   } catch (err: unknown) {
166:     throwResolutionError('resolveGraphSeed', seed, err);
167:   }
168: 
169:   return {
170:     filePath: '<unknown>',
171:     startLine: 1,
172:     endLine: 1,
173:     symbolId: seed.symbolId,
174:     fqName: null,
175:     kind: null,
176:     confidence: 0.1,
177:     resolution: 'file_anchor',
178:     score: null,
179:     snippet: null,
180:     range: null,
181:     provider: 'graph',
182:     source: seed.source,
183:   };
184: }
185: 
186: /** Resolve a single seed to an ArtifactRef */
187: export function resolveSeed(seed: CodeGraphSeed): ArtifactRef {
188:   try {
189:     const d = graphDb.getDb();
190: 
191:     // Try exact symbol match at line
192:     if (seed.startLine) {
193:       const exact = d.prepare(`
194:         SELECT * FROM code_nodes
195:         WHERE file_path = ? AND start_line = ?

199: 
200:       if (exact) {
201:         return {
202:           filePath: seed.filePath,
203:           startLine: exact.start_line as number,
204:           endLine: exact.end_line as number,
205:           symbolId: exact.symbol_id as string,
206:           fqName: exact.fq_name as string,
207:           kind: exact.kind as string,
208:           confidence: 1.0,
209:           resolution: 'exact',
210:           score: null,
211:           snippet: null,
212:           range: seed.startLine
213:             ? { start: seed.startLine, end: seed.endLine ?? seed.startLine }
214:             : null,
215:           provider: 'code_graph',
216:           source: seed.source,
217:         };
218:       }
219: 
220:       const nearExact = d.prepare(`

224:         LIMIT 1

227:       if (nearExact) {

234:           fqName: nearExact.fq_name as string,
235:           kind: nearExact.kind as string,
236:           confidence: Math.max(0, 0.95 - (distance * 0.02)),
237:           resolution: 'near_exact',
238:           score: null,
239:           snippet: null,
240:           range: seed.startLine

281: 
282: function stampContextTraceTruncation(
283:   sections: GraphContextSection[],
284:   reason: NonNullable<ContextWhyIncluded['truncationReason']>,
285: ): void {
286:   for (const section of sections) {
287:     if (!section.why_included) {

297: /** Generate a one-line summary of the resolved context */

530: /** Run indexFiles with a timeout guard */
531: async function indexWithTimeout(
532:   config: IndexerConfig,
533:   timeoutMs: number,
534:   indexOptions?: Parameters<typeof indexFiles>[1],
535: ): Promise<void> {
536:   const controller = new AbortController();
537:   const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);
538: 
539:   try {
540:     const results = await Promise.race([
541:       // Pass the deadline signal so indexFiles' phase runner can stop
542:       // between phases on timeout instead of running to completion in the
543:       // background and discarding the result.
544:       indexFiles(config, { ...indexOptions, signal: controller.signal }),
545:       new Promise<never>((_, reject) => {
546:         controller.signal.addEventListener('abort', () =>
547:           reject(new Error(`Auto-indexing timed out after ${timeoutMs}ms`)),
548:         );
549:       }),
550:     ]);
551: 
552:     let persistedDetectorProvenance: ReturnType<typeof graphDb.getLastDetectorProvenance> = null;
553:     for (const result of results) {
554:       try {
555:         persistIndexedFileResult(result);
556:         persistedDetectorProvenance ??= result.detectorProvenance;
557:       } catch {
558:         // Best-effort: skip files that fail to persist.  File remains stale
559:         // (mtime=0 from Stage 1) so the next scan will retry structural rows.
560:       }
561:     }
562: 
563:     if (persistedDetectorProvenance) {
564:       graphDb.setLastDetectorProvenance(persistedDetectorProvenance);
565:     }
566:   } finally {
567:     clearTimeout(timer);
568:   }
569: }
570: 
571: /**
572:  * Persist one indexed file without marking it fresh until structural rows land.
573:  *
574:  * Stage `file_mtime_ms=0`, write nodes/edges, then finalize with the real
575:  * mtime so persistence failures leave the file stale for retry.
576:  *
577:  * Wrap the four storage operations (stage upsert, replaceNodes, replaceEdges,
578:  * finalize upsert) in a single per-file transaction so a crash mid-persistence
579:  * rolls all four back atomically. Per-file scope keeps the
580:  * lock window short (3-4 statements) so concurrent readers/writers on the
581:  * same DB are not starved during a long scan.
582:  */
583: export function persistIndexedFileResult(
584:   result: ParseResult,
585:   opts: { deferDanglingTargetPrune?: boolean } = {},
586: ): void {
587:   if (result.parseHealth === 'error') {
588:     graphDb.recordParseDiagnostic(result.filePath, result.parseErrors.join('; '));
589:     return;
590:   }
591: 
592:   // Atomic per-file persistence boundary.
593:   const tx = graphDb.getDb().transaction(() => {
594:     const fileId = graphDb.upsertFile(
595:       result.filePath, result.language, result.contentHash,
596:       result.nodes.length, result.edges.length,
597:       result.parseHealth, result.parseDurationMs,
598:       { fileMtimeMs: 0 },
599:     );
600:     graphDb.replaceNodes(fileId, result.nodes);
601:     const sourceIds = result.nodes.map((node) => node.symbolId);
602:     graphDb.replaceEdges(sourceIds, result.edges, opts);
603:     graphDb.upsertFile(
604:       result.filePath, result.language, result.contentHash,
605:       result.nodes.length, result.edges.length,
606:       result.parseHealth, result.parseDurationMs,
607:     );
608:     graphDb.clearParseDiagnostic(result.filePath);
609:   });

300: /** Resolve any seed variant to an ArtifactRef */
301: function resolveAnySeed(seed: AnySeed): ArtifactRef {
302:   if (isManualSeed(seed)) return resolveManualSeed(seed);

307: /** Resolve multiple seeds, deduplicate overlapping refs */
308: export function resolveSeeds(seeds: AnySeed[]): ArtifactRef[] {
309:   const refsByKey = new Map<string, ArtifactRef>();
310: 
311:   const compareRefs = (left: ArtifactRef, right: ArtifactRef): number => (
312:     (right.score ?? Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY) - (left.score ?? Number.NEGATIVE_INFINITY)
313:     || right.confidence - left.confidence
314:     || left.filePath.localeCompare(right.filePath)
315:     || left.startLine - right.startLine
316:   );
317: 
318:   for (const seed of seeds) {
319:     const ref = resolveAnySeed(seed);
320:     const key = ref.symbolId ?? `${ref.filePath}:${ref.startLine}`;
321:     const previous = refsByKey.get(key);
322:     if (!previous || compareRefs(previous, ref) > 0) {
323:       refsByKey.set(key, ref);
324:     }
325:   }
326: 
327:   return [...refsByKey.values()].sort(compareRefs);
328: }

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // [mk-code-index-launcher] MCP child-process launcher for the mk-code-index server
3: // (system-code-graph). Loads project-local env overrides, applies the maintainer-mode
4: // INDEX_* override when SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true, auto-migrates the
5: // code-graph database from the former shared location (.opencode/.spec-kit/code-graph/database/)
6: // back to the skill-local location (mcp_server/database/), ensures dist
7: // artifacts are built and current, serializes concurrent starts via a filesystem bootstrap
8: // lock, sets SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR for the child process (operator override wins),
9: // then spawns the code-graph MCP server child. All stderr lines are tagged with the
10: // bracketed module prefix for ops grepping. See .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/ for
11: // the standalone skill that owns the server source.
12: 
13: 'use strict';
14: 
15: const fs = require('fs');
16: const path = require('path');
17: const { spawn, spawnSync } = require('child_process');
18: const { createSessionProxy, createClassifyFrame } = require('./lib/launcher-session-proxy.cjs');
19: 
20: const root = path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '..');
21: const opencodeDir = path.join(root, '.opencode');
22: const BLOCKED_CHILD_ENV_RE = /^(NODE_|npm_|NPM_)/;
23: const DOTENV_ALLOW_RE = /^(SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_|SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN$|MK_CODE_INDEX_)/;
24: 
25: // Load project-local env overrides BEFORE spawning the MCP child. .env.local wins over
26: // .env, both are gitignored. Existing process.env wins over file values (do not override).
27: // Minimal parser — no external dependency.
28: function loadEnvFile(filePath) {
29:   if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return 0;
30:   let count = 0;
31:   const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
32:   for (const line of raw.split('\n')) {
33:     const trimmed = line.trim();
34:     if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith('#')) continue;
35:     const m = trimmed.match(/^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.*)$/);
36:     if (!m) continue;
37:     const key = m[1];
38:     let val = m[2];
39:     // Strip matching outer quotes (single or double)
40:     if ((val.startsWith('"') && val.endsWith('"')) || (val.startsWith("'") && val.endsWith("'"))) {
41:       val = val.slice(1, -1);
42:     }
43:     // The minimal parser is line-oriented; embedded \n in a quoted value would
44:     // already have terminated the line, but defend explicitly.
45:     if (val.includes('\n') || val.includes('\0')) {
46:       process.stderr.write(`[mk-code-index-launcher] env value for ${key} contains control chars; skipping\n`);
47:       continue;
48:     }
49:     if (!DOTENV_ALLOW_RE.test(key)) {
50:       process.stderr.write(`[mk-code-index-launcher] env ${key} from ${path.basename(filePath)} is not allowlisted; skipping\n`);
51:       continue;
52:     }
53:     if (!(key in process.env)) {
54:       process.env[key] = val;
55:       count++;
56:     }
57:   }
58:   return count;
59: }
60:

</content>

51: function loadEnvFile(filePath) {
52:   if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) return 0;
53:   let count = 0;
54:   const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
55:   for (const line of raw.split('\n')) {
56:     const trimmed = line.trim();
57:     if (!trimmed || trimmed.startsWith('#')) continue;
58:     const m = trimmed.match(/^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*=\s*(.*)$/);
59:     if (!m) continue;
60:     const key = m[1];
61:     let val = m[2];
62:     // Strip matching outer quotes (single or double)
63:     if ((val.startsWith('"') && val.endsWith('"')) || (val.startsWith("'") && val.endsWith("'"))) {
64:       val = val.slice(1, -1);
65:     }
66:     if (!(key in process.env)) {
67:       process.env[key] = val;
68:       count++;
69:     }
70:   }

---

### 🔍 Code Graph

The Code Graph is an indexed relationship map of your code, so the assistant answers structural questions straight from real call and import edges instead of guessing from text: "what calls this?", "what imports this?", "what breaks if we change it?"

The intended routing order is graph-first: the Code Graph resolves structural queries first, then Memory supports session decisions and active-task context after the packet-local recovery sources have been checked. A 3-tier FTS fallback escalates automatically when results are weak.

 
#### Default Scope (End-User Code Only)

By default, code-graph scans your repo code only. Five `.opencode/` folders are excluded so end-user search results stay signal-rich:

- `.opencode/skills/**`
- `.opencode/agents/**`
- `.opencode/commands/**`
- `<active-spec-folder>/**`
- `.opencode/plugins/**`

Maintainers can opt folders back in process-wide with env vars:

display.text:
---

### 🔍 Code Graph

The Code Graph is an indexed relationship map of your code, so the assistant answers structural questions straight from real call and import edges instead of guessing from text: "what calls this?", "what imports this?", "what breaks if we change it?"

The intended routing order is graph-first: the Code Graph resolves structural queries first, then Memory supports session decisions and active-task context after the packet-local recovery sources have been checked. A 3-tier FTS fallback escalates automatically when results are weak.

 
#### Default Scope (End-User Code Only)

By default, code-graph scans your repo code only. Five `.opencode/` folders are excluded so end-user search results stay signal-rich:

- `.opencode/skills/**`
- `.opencode/agents/**`
- `.opencode/commands/**`
- `<active-spec-folder>/**`
- `.opencode/plugins/**`

Maintainers can opt folders back in process-wide with env vars:

```bash
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS=true       # all skills
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS=sk-x,sk-y  # only listed skills (csv)
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS=true
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS=true
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS=true
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS=true
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR=/path/to/code-graph-db # optional DB-dir override
```

Per-call args override env vars when provided. Env vars apply only for fields omitted from the scan call:

```ts
code_graph_scan({

### Maintainer-Mode Code-Graph Flags (already disabled for end users)

All 4 runtime MCP configs (`opencode.json`, `.claude/mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, `.vscode/mcp.json`) carry five opt-in maintainer flags:

```text
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS    (covers .opencode/skills/**)
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS    (covers .opencode/agents/**)
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS  (covers .opencode/commands/**)
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS     (covers <active-spec-folder>/**)
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS   (covers .opencode/plugins/**)
SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR          (optional code-graph SQLite directory override)
```

display.text: "sequential_thinking": { "type": "local" } } } ```   ### Maintainer-Mode Code-Graph Flags (already disabled for end users) All 4 runtime MCP configs (`opencode.json`, `.claude/mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, `.vscode/mcp.json`) carry five opt-in maintainer flags: ```text SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS (covers .opencode/skills/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS (covers .opencode/agents/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS (covers .opencode/commands/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS (covers /**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS (covers .opencode/plugins/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR (optional code-graph SQLite directory override) ``` **End users see all 5 as `"false"`** thanks to the [git clean filter](#git-clean-filter--maintainer-mode-stays-local). That's the framework default and what you want, the code graph indexes your project code, not the framework backend. **Maintainers (us) have all 5 as `"true"`** locally because we navigate `.opencode/` to iterate on the framework. The smudge filter restores `"true"` on checkout/pull/clone after running `./scripts/setup-maintainer-filters.sh`. **Per-call override:** the same five flags exist as `includeSkills` / `includeAgents` / `includeCommands` / `includeSpecs` / `includePlugins` arguments on `code_graph_scan`. Per-call args always override env defaults, so you can flip behavior for one scan without editing config. #### Git clean filter: maintainer mode stays local

245: ## 8. TROUBLESHOOTING
246: 
247: | What You See | Cause | Fix |
248: |---|---|---|
249: | Launcher exits with "standalone-storage guard violation" | `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR` resolves outside the workspace. | Use a path inside the repo, or unset the override. |
250: | `requiredAction: "code_graph_scan"` in tool responses | Graph is stale, missing, or scope-mismatched. | Run `code_graph_scan` with the intended scope. Use `incremental: false` for scope changes. |
251: | Skill files do not appear in scan results | `.opencode/skills/**` is excluded by default. | Set `includeSkills: true` per-call, or set `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS=true` (or enable maintainer mode). |
252: | `parserHealth: "quarantined"` | Parser failures were added to the skip-list. | Inspect `parserSkipList.sample` from `code_graph_status`, then repair or accept the quarantine. |
253: | Unknown MCP tool error mentions `mk-code-index` | Tool name not registered in `mcp_server/tools/code-graph-tools.ts`. | Add the schema, handler export, and dispatch case in one change. |
254: | Config still uses `system_code_graph` namespace | Pre-packet-010 config not updated. | Rename the entry to `mk_code_index`, point at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs`, and use `mcp__mk_code_index__*` for all tool references. |
255: | Root-level `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/dist/` exists | Stale pre-cleanup build output is still present. | Run `npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run clean && npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run build`. |
256: | `/doctor:mcp debug --server mk_code_index` fails `root_dist_absent` | Doctor detected stale root-level build output. | Run `/doctor:mcp debug --server mk_code_index --fix`, or run the clean and build commands above manually. |
257: | Cross-runtime config audit needed | Want to verify checked-in runtimes are consistent. | `for f in opencode.json .claude/mcp.json .codex/config.toml .vscode/mcp.json; do printf '%-30s ' "$f"; grep -c 'mk_code_index\|mk-code-index' "$f"; done` should show >= 1 hit per file. |
258: | `/doctor:mcp install --server mk_code_index` fails with "Unknown server" | `doctor_mcp_install.yaml` was not updated to include `mk_code_index` in `valid_values`. | Run `/doctor:update` to refresh subsystem coverage, or patch `valid_values` directly. |
259:

metadata.preview:
### Indexing scope flags

| Flag | Default | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/skills/**`. Also accepts comma-separated `sk-*` allowlist (e.g. `sk-doc,sk-git`). |
| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/agents/**`. |
| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/commands/**`. |
| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS` | `false` | Include `<active-spec-folder>/**`. |
| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/plugins/**`. |

End-user defaults are all `false` so the indexer is quiet and the SQLite file stays small. Per-call `code_graph_scan` arguments (`includeSkills`, `includeAgents`, `includeCommands`, `includeSpecs`, `includePlugins`) override the env defaults for one-shot scans.

### Maintainer mode

Maintainers who index the full repo set:

```bash
echo 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true' >> .env.local
```

display.text:
### Indexing scope flags

| Flag | Default | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/skills/**`. Also accepts comma-separated `sk-*` allowlist (e.g. `sk-doc,sk-git`). |
| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/agents/**`. |
| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/commands/**`. |
| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS` | `false` | Include `<active-spec-folder>/**`. |
| `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS` | `false` | Include `.opencode/plugins/**`. |

End-user defaults are all `false` so the indexer is quiet and the SQLite file stays small. Per-call `code_graph_scan` arguments (`includeSkills`, `includeAgents`, `includeCommands`, `includeSpecs`, `includePlugins`) override the env defaults for one-shot scans.

### Maintainer mode

Maintainers who index the full repo set:

```bash
echo 'SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true' >> .env.local
```

`.env.local` is gitignored. The launcher reads it on startup, and when the flag is `true` it forces all 5 `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_*` flags to `true` before spawning the server, overriding any committed `false` values from the runtime config. Per-call scan args still override.

---

## 8. TROUBLESHOOTING

| What You See | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| Launcher exits with "standalone-storage guard violation" | `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR` resolves outside the workspace. | Use a path inside the repo, or unset the override. |
| `requiredAction: "code_graph_scan"` in tool responses | Graph is stale, missing, or scope-mismatched. | Run `code_graph_scan` with the intended scope. Use `incremental: false` for scope changes. |
| Skill files do not appear in scan results | `.opencode/skills/**` is excluded by default. | Set `includeSkills: true` per-call, or set `SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS=true` (or enable maintainer mode). |
| `parserHealth: "quarantined"` | Parser failures were added to the skip-list. | Inspect `parserSkipList.sample` from `code_graph_status`, then repair or accept the quarantine. |
| Unknown MCP tool error mentions `mk-code-index` | Tool name not registered in `mcp_server/tools/code-graph-tools.ts`. | Add the schema, handler export, and dispatch case in one change. |
| Config still uses `system_code_graph` namespace | Pre-packet-010 config not updated. | Rename the entry to `mk_code_index`, point at `.opencode/bin/mk-code-index-launcher.cjs`, and use `mcp__mk_code_index__*` for all tool references. |
| Root-level `.opencode/skills/system-code-graph/dist/` exists | Stale pre-cleanup build output is still present. | Run `npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run clean && npm --prefix .opencode/skills/system-code-graph run build`. |
| `/doctor:mcp debug --server mk_code_index` fails `root_dist_absent` | Doctor detected stale root-level build output. | Run `/doctor:mcp debug --server mk_code_index --fix`, or run the clean and build commands above manually. |

metadata.preview: All 4 runtime MCP configs (`opencode.json`, `.claude/mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, `.vscode/mcp.json`) carry five opt-in maintainer flags: ```text SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS (covers .opencode/skills/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS (covers .opencode/agents/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS (covers .opencode/commands/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS (covers /**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS (covers .opencode/plugins/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR (optional code-graph SQLite directory override) ``` **End users see all 5 as `"false"`** thanks to the [git clean filter](#git-clean-filter--maintainer-mode-stays-local). That's the framework default and what you want, the code graph indexes your project code, not the framework backend. **Maintainers (us) have all 5 as `"true"`** locally because we navigate `.opencode/` to iterate on the framework. The smudge filter restores `"true"` on checkout/pull/clone after running `./scripts/setup-maintainer-filters.sh`. **Per-call override:** the same five flags exist as `includeSkills` / `includeAgents` / `includeCommands` / `includeSpecs` / `includePlugins` arguments on `code_graph_scan`. Per-call args always override env defaults, so you can flip behavior for one scan without editing config.

display.text: All 4 runtime MCP configs (`opencode.json`, `.claude/mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, `.vscode/mcp.json`) carry five opt-in maintainer flags: ```text SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SKILLS (covers .opencode/skills/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_AGENTS (covers .opencode/agents/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_COMMANDS (covers .opencode/commands/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_SPECS (covers /**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_INDEX_PLUGINS (covers .opencode/plugins/**) SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR (optional code-graph SQLite directory override) ``` **End users see all 5 as `"false"`** thanks to the [git clean filter](#git-clean-filter--maintainer-mode-stays-local). That's the framework default and what you want, the code graph indexes your project code, not the framework backend. **Maintainers (us) have all 5 as `"true"`** locally because we navigate `.opencode/` to iterate on the framework. The smudge filter restores `"true"` on checkout/pull/clone after running `./scripts/setup-maintainer-filters.sh`. **Per-call override:** the same five flags exist as `includeSkills` / `includeAgents` / `includeCommands` / `includeSpecs` / `includePlugins` arguments on `code_graph_scan`. Per-call args always override env defaults, so you can flip behavior for one scan without editing config.
